Okay, welcome back. So let's just jump right in where we left off. So what I'm gonna do is I'm gonna pull up on the screen here The campaigns that we've been working with in this training. Okay, but let's now assume something's not working. Okay And so this is the actual checklist that you can go through the good news about this is that assuming stuff isn't working It's usually not everything. It's usually just one of these little things and we just fix the one little thing and everything's cool The trick, you know, if we want to call it a trick The trick is to find that one little thing that needs fixing it It's real easy if you know what to look for and you keep a level head and you leave emotions completely out of the equation so you don't have to go through this checklist and less Everything that we just talked about in the last video is is relevant You know, so like if it's only been running for three days or something There's no point in going through this checklist, you know, if you haven't been getting a lot of traffic There's no point in getting going through this checklist But if you have and stuff quits working or whatever, this is this is how to diagnose it Okay, so thing number one is we want to look to see if people are actually clicking on your ad, you know That's important. Let's say you spend a thousand bucks and nobody bought anything Well, and you know, your tech stack is working and all of that kind of stuff Maybe no one's clicking the day ad so before you go and assume that your website sucks or that people don't want your product or whatever Let's make sure they're clicking. So if you remember we built that little demo view Earlier where I've showed you how to like customize what Facebook shows you here So I've just got like a couple of days running. I can't remember when we built like these newer campaigns here But what like let's assume actually let's just look at one of the ones where we've spent more money like this one All right, so this one actually is a newer campaign This is one of our level two campaigns and let's assume this one was doing very badly Which is not doing particularly great. I mean according to Facebook. It's almost a break even so that's not too shabby But let's just say it was you know, we're not happy with it Well, the first thing we want to do is we want to say all right are people actually clicking All right, and so in this case they are but if they were not What we would want to do is we'd want to look and say, okay, well what the heck is going on here Why aren't they clicking and if we have a really low? CTR like all of these are actually relatively low click-through rates. I like to see over 1% here So if this is really low and it's not that great then you could immediately be like I'm pretty sure the problem is the ad Nobody's clicking on it. I'm not using the right combination of words and pictures Right and again if we're doing this We always have to follow that principle which is hey, you don't judge at the campaign level you judge at the ad set level So in this case, you know, let's say that they weren't clicking and this was really horrible or whatever I could look and I could be like alright. Is it universally bad in this case? No, it's not universally bad I've got some audiences that up. They're actually clicking a little bit better than others now in this case We're so close to break even and we don't have a lot of data that I'm not gonna You know make any decisions based on this. I just want to show you so if you have a really low Unique outbound click-through rate and remember that's all that matters, right? So there's a difference between clicks and Facebook and the link clicks and unique outbound clicks all we want to measure Are unique outbound clicks? That's where people are leaving it going your website and clicking on your ads and stuff Okay, so if this is low and you know That's the issue. Well, we already know all right. That's probably what's going on So you don't have to go change your entire business. Okay, so the next thing we want to do is we want to look and see if Your cost per thousand impressions also known as your CPM is Unusually high and this affects everything. So this is how you pay for stuff in Facebook and you can find it right Here, let's see. I think I have it in this particular view. Oh, I didn't set it up here You know, so let's edit this here. So what I'm gonna do is click on customize columns I'm gonna type in CPM what showing that and of course dyslexia is not really helpful. Here we go Boom. All right, so I want to add this in To my stats and I'll just have it come on Facebook cooperate with me, please kindly There we go. I think I'll have this here That's just the order in which it's shown. All right, and so I'm gonna save as preset I'm gonna leave it as the same preset and click apply and now it just updates this. All right, so There we go. All right, so okay if my cost per click Let's say I'm getting plenty of clicks my click through rate is good But my you know cost per lead is too high or my cost per customer is too high before I get weirded out and think Oh, man, it's my copy. Everything sucks I need to make sure I'm not paying unusually high cost per 1000 impressions and this is what drives everything really in Facebook, you know, so if this was a hundred bucks per thousand impressions My my cost per click would be three times more. All right, so you always want to look at this now in this case This is this is okay, you know, but if it was ten bucks last week and this week is $30 I know something's probably weird here and Facebook's either, you know, it's an auction based system So maybe the prices have gone up or Maybe they want me to refresh the creative So if you've been running an ad for a long time and your cost per thousand impressions is keep going up That's usually Facebook's way of saying dude We're gonna keep charging you more until you change your ad because we don't want you to be showing the same ad every time All right, so like if that has happened then you're like, oh, okay. Wait a minute. Everything's not horrible the cost per thousand impressions has gone up and We probably just need to change the the ad so that is what you would do in that particular case So now let's go on to the next one which is assuming let's say, you know, you're using a legion funnel and So they're clicking. All right, you're getting a decent click through rate. Your cost per click is okay. Your cost per 1000 Impressions is okay. All that looks good All right, and you know ultimately at the end of the day, you're not getting any money or whatever So you need to see if they're opting in All right, so this is if you're going to, you know, a legion funnel or whatever if they're not Doing that if they're going to your opt-in page, right? And at the end of the day, you're not selling stuff, but it all begins with them opting in Right, and they're not opting in then we know that it's probably not your offer. We know it's not your ad they're clicking All right, it's probably not the copy or what you're saying on your webinar or your video sales letter It's your opt-in page. You got to fix your opt-in page. So that's where you would Put your attention, you know And then you let it run and then you would see if everything gets fixed and if it doesn't you would then now go through this All right, and this is again specifically for this one section of your diagnostic checklist Is if your campaign or your sales process starts with somebody opting in okay Let's say that to stick with that theme Let's say that they are that you are running just a classic, you know Internet marketing sales process for you drive people for would add to an opt-in page and now they go through a funnel But they're they're not buying right, but they are opting in well One thing you got to do is you want to make sure they're they're opening your follow-up emails Right because of course they're most of them aren't going to buy immediately right after they opt in that's normal We talked about the law of damnits, right? That's why we have a super system and that's why we have all that other cool stuff So if they're not opening those emails Then they're probably not going back to the site All right, but let's say they are opening the emails So you're like okay, that's good now by the way if they're not opening the emails What's the factor that causes people to open it's a subject line right so in this case you would just be like okay I just need to change the subject lines You would change your subject lines and then you'd let everything run for a little while and if it Resolves itself great. You're cool. You know you didn't have to rebuild your entire business You just change your subject lines, but if you're still not selling stuff in this particular example now it's time to be like okay Are they clicking the damn links in the emails? Right and so if they are Then you would move on to the next step But if they're not if you're looking at your email thingy and your stats are you they're saying hey You know you have a good open rate, but there you know one's clicking What causes people to click? It's the copy in the email right so in this case you would just change the copy in your emails You would make it more aggressive or more benefit laden or you like tell them hey click here right now You know and you would do that and so you would fix that one little thing And then you would let the campaign run for another 72 hours another week or whatever And if it resolves and you start selling stuff sweet, but if it doesn't now you're like okay So let's say all that stuff is cool right and that you know They're they're going to your sales page and so now like let's say you you you're not using a lead gen Campaign you where you send them to an opt-in page and then they ultimately see your offer Let's say you're doing like what I'm doing in this in this particular You know campaign that we have used For all of our demonstrations so far, which is I'm driving people straight to a sales page right so Ultimately if everything else is in place and they're actually going to your sales page But you're not selling anything the first thing you need to do is you need to see if they're clicking to your order form Right, so if they aren't clicking to your order form then we can already understand it. Okay Sometimes we're on the sales page. All right, but let's say they are Clicking to your order form, but they're not buying that means your ad works and you know in the event that you're getting the leads first That means your opt-in page is cool. Your emails are cool everything like your sales page is cool, but Something's wrong with your order form right and so instead of redoing everything, you know like oh, I just need to change the copy on my Order form maybe change the layout right just mess around that so like that is your bottleneck It's just the dang order form not the entire business. So in this case you would change your order form copy and One other thing, you know the final thing here is if you're using a two-step order form Like I do and let me show you that specifically what I mean by the way So over here in your click funnels if you will it's what I'm using for this thing. So I think This will redirect to an expired page. Oh, maybe not awesome. Okay, cool. So if you click this all right hang on just a sec There we go, sorry about that if you click this you got to put your name and email and phone number in Before you're taken to the credit card part right so if you've got yours set up this way You need to look to see okay. Are people actually doing that first step? You know and if they're not then you're like wait a minute Everything's not broken. I just need to fix whatever the heck is wrong here. Maybe it's the copy. It's the layout Maybe you know getting rid of the phone number in this particular case would increase conversion there, right? So that's how you diagnose all this stuff to ultimately get to all right. Are they buying or not? Usually what our brains want to do is they want to go okay. I ran the ad they're not buying It's the ad and my entire business sucks But in reality, there's all these other little things that lead up to purchase and usually it's not all of them That are broken. It's usually just one of them Right and so if you keep this checklist in mind When things get wonky and you take all the emotion out of it and you go through this checklist using enough data I mean you don't start an ad yesterday and then look at it today After you got a hundred clicks or whatever and you freak yourself out, you know, you give it enough time to have enough data If you do that Everything's gonna be easier for you and things are gonna work better and you're gonna risk a lot less money And you're gonna make more because you're going to find out exactly what needs fixing and you're gonna fix that one thing instead of Rebuilding everything which is basically like totally starting from scratch every single time is very very frustrating All right So I wanted to give you all this stuff first before we start building level three campaigns and before we continue our scaling mission Because you know as we spend more money in this stuff It eventually, you know, something's gonna break right and this is how to deal with it. Okay, so cool Thanks for watching this keep this handy. Of course, I've got this checklist here as available to you as a download and Always use it because this is this is how you make your decisions, you know without emotion This is gonna keep you from from messing up and that's where we lose money is when we mess up We don't want to do that. We want to make money. Okay. I'll see you in the next video
